headline: Sarawak Palm Oil Mills Discharge Exceeds BOD Limits at 34% of Sites
context: DOE Sarawak's 2024 compliance audit found 34% of palm oil mills exceeded biological oxygen demand discharge limits, up from 22% in 2020.

cards.big: Palm oil mills are the largest point-source water polluters in Sarawak.
cards.sub: 34% exceeded legal BOD limits in 2024 — and the compliance rate is getting worse, not better.

cards.big: Palm oil mill effluent has a BOD 100 times higher than domestic sewage.
cards.sub: When discharged above limits, it strips dissolved oxygen from rivers within a 15km radius.

cards.big: Average fine for BOD exceedance was RM12,000 in 2024.
cards.sub: Treatment upgrades cost RM2-5M per mill. Non-compliance is 200 times cheaper than compliance.
14 non-compliant mills held active RSPO certificates, raising questions about certification auditing.

cards.big: When breaking the law costs RM12,000 and following it costs RM2M, the law has set its own price.
Effluent standards work when fines exceed savings from non-compliance. Malaysia's fine structure was set in 1974. Updating it would cost nothing and change everything.
